titles.title: A Data Persistence Architecture for the SimJulia Framework

citation.unstructured_citation: SimPy, a process-based discrete-event simulation framework based on standard Python, Klaus Müller, Tony Vignaux, Stefan Scherfke and Lünsdorf, Ontje, \urlhttps://simpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/, Online; accessed 07-September-2023

ResumableFunctions: C# Sharp Style Generators for Julia, Lauwens, Ben, Journal of Open Source Software, 2, 18, 400, 2017

Bridging the Object-Relational Divide: ORM Technologies Can Simplify Data Access, But Be Aware Of The Challenges That Come With Introducing This New Layer Of Abstraction, Russell, Craig, Queue, 6, 3, 18–28, 2008, ACM New York, NY, USA

HTTP.jl: HTTP client and server functionality for Julia, Web technologies in the Julia Programming Language, JuliaWeb:, \urlhttps://github.com/JuliaWeb/HTTP.jl, Online; accessed 14-September-2023

Simedis 2.0: on the road toward a comprehensive mass casualty incident medical management simulator, De Rouck, Ruben and Debacker, Michel and Hubloue, Ives and Koghee, Selma and Van Utterbeeck, Filip and Dhondt, Erwin, 2018 Winter Simulation Conference (WSC), 2713–2724, 2018, IEEE

Agent Based Modelling and Simulation tools: A review of the state-of-art software, Abar, Sameera and Theodoropoulos, Georgios K and Lemarinier, Pierre and O’Hare, Gregory MP, Computer Science Review, 24, 13–33, 2017, Elsevier

Manpower planning using simulation and heuristic optimization, Abdessameud, Oussama Mazari and Van Kerckhoven, Johan and Van Utterbeeck, Filip and Guerry, Marie-Anne, 2019 Industrial Simulation Conference, 53–58, 2019, EUROSIS-ETI
